name: Unity Productions Foundation
mission: In this 3-hour series for TV & streaming, a young Muslim Couple travels along historic Route 66, discovering their American roots and the many stories of Muslims enriching America. Along the way, they learn how Muslims are a part of American spirituality, food, technology, music and much more.
programs: The Great American Muslim Road Trip
UPF Film - The Sultan and the Saint
UPF Film - American Muslims: Facts vs. Fiction
theoryOfChange: Unity Productions Foundation (UPF) has a published strategic framework outlined in its GuideStar profile under 'Goals & Strategy' and on its website under 'Mission: Peace and Pluralism'. The organization's mission is to counter bigotry and create peace through media by increasing understanding and dialogue among spiritual and cultural traditions. Their key strategies include producing documentary films for broadcast and online viewing, implementing long-term educational campaigns, and engaging with Hollywood through their MOST (Muslims on Screen and Television) Resource Center to provide facts and research to scriptwriters and producers. UPF also partners with interfaith groups to host dialogues nationwide. The intended impact is to empower citizens with greater understanding, nourish pluralism, and break down stereotypes.
scoreSummary: Unity Productions Foundation shows good impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Evaluations of 20,000 Dialogues demonstrate power to break down stereotypes.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

zakat.claim_evidence: Unity Productions Foundation's donation pages and organizational descriptions detail various methods of giving, including online donations, mail, bank wire, stocks, IRA funds, and donor-advised funds, but do not explicitly mention 'zakat' or any dedicated zakat funds or campaigns. UPF conducts teacher workshops and produces educational guides to counter media caricatures.
The organization facilitates programs like MOST to influence how Muslims are depicted on screen.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization is classified as sadaqah-eligible and serves as a medium fit for donors interested in media advocacy and social justice [8]. With an alignment score of 29/50, it addresses the urgent cause of countering bigotry through long-term cultural shifts rather than immediate humanitarian relief [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: UPF reported a total revenue of $3,774,653 and maintains a program expense ratio of 76.1%, indicating that the majority of funds support its media and educational missions [1][2]. While the organization achieves significant global viewership and conducts extensive teacher workshops, its focus on systemic change and advocacy results in a moderate impact score of 33/50 [3][6].

The organization operates diverse programs including 'Muslims on Screen and Television' (MOST) and 'The Great American Muslim Road Trip' to foster cultural understanding [3][5].

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="4">97.5/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="10">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
UPF operates lean development efforts, spending only <cite id="11">$0.22 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
With <cite id="9">13.1 months of working capital</cite> held in working capital, the charity is well-positioned to manage long-term media projects.

strengths_deep_dive: Unity Productions Foundation distinguishes itself through a systemic change model that targets the root of social bias. By engaging directly with the entertainment industry through the <cite id="3">MOST project</cite>, they influence the narratives seen by millions. Their educational impact is documented through the distribution of <cite id="12">video lessons and discussion guides</cite> used in academic settings. Furthermore, the organization maintains high governance standards with <cite id="13">independent board oversight</cite> and a clear conflict of interest policy.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1999, Unity Productions Foundation (UPF) is a specialized media nonprofit dedicated to <cite id="1">countering bigotry and stereotypical depictions of Muslims</cite> in film and television. The organization operates at the intersection of media advocacy and education, producing high-quality documentaries and television initiatives designed to <cite id="2">foster cultural understanding and dialogue</cite> among diverse spiritual traditions. UPF's strategic approach includes the <cite id="3">Muslims on Screen and Television (MOST) project</cite>, which provides resources to Hollywood writers and producers to ensure more accurate Muslim characters. Financially, the organization maintains a <cite id="4">97.5/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability. In 2023, UPF reported <cite id="5">total revenue of $2,774,622</cite>, with a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 76.1%</cite>. Beyond production, the charity extends its impact through educational outreach, having conducted <cite id="7">25 teacher workshops</cite> to integrate its film content into classroom curricula. While the organization faces a <cite id="8">3-year revenue CAGR of -36.7%</cite>, it maintains a robust safety net with <cite id="9">13.1 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, ensuring operational stability during production cycles.
